title: Hades Visits!
tagline: Uncle Hades has never seen sunlight. He has emerged from the underworld for the first time in forty years. He has THOUGHTS about sunlight.

guest.name: Uncle Hades
guest.role: Zeus's other uncle. Ruler of the underworld. Perfectly pleasant. Has simply been underground for four decades and finds the surface world aggressively bright, aggressively warm, and aggressively cheerful. He does not hate it. He is just very, very overwhelmed.
guest.visual: Tall thin god, dark purple toga, silver crown with tiny skull motifs (very small, not scary), very pale, large round glasses he put on the moment he hit surface level — the sun is A LOT. Carries a small black dog (Cerberus puppy, three heads, all adorable). Speaks formally. Means well entirely.
guest.personality: Not sinister — deeply formal, slightly tragic-poetic about ordinary things, genuinely delighted by his nephew Zeus. Finds everything on Olympus very loud and very bright. The three-headed puppy is named Fluffy by Zeus on the spot.

characters.problem: Hades accidentally brought a small piece of underworld fog with him. It seeps through the lowest rooms of Olympus, making everything slightly grey and dramatic. He didn't notice. He thought the atmosphere was an improvement.

scenes.title: The Arrival
scenes.narration: Uncle Hades arrived at dawn. He had sent a message three weeks ahead. The message said he would arrive at dawn and that everyone should prepare for his arrival in a manner befitting the lord of the underworld. Zeus had told everyone it was a family visit. Hades emerged from a shadow at the base of Olympus, looked at the sunrise, put on very large round glasses, said 'extraordinary,' and stood completely still for forty-five seconds processing the sky.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. UNCLE HADES emerging from a shadow at Olympus's base — tall, thin, dark purple toga, silver crown with tiny (cute, not scary) skull motifs, very pale. He looks up at the sunrise. He slowly puts on enormous round dark glasses. He says 'extraordinary' to nobody. He stands frozen looking at the sky. Little Zeus stands nearby watching him, crown slightly crooked, expression of genuine affection. A small three-headed puppy trots out of the shadow behind Hades. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Puppy
scenes.narration: Zeus noticed the three-headed puppy immediately and named all three heads before Hades finished his sentence about the puppy's formal underworld designation. Hades said: 'He is the Keeper of the—' Zeus said: 'Fluffy.' Hades said: 'His designation is—' Zeus said: 'Fluffy, Fluffy, and also Fluffy.' The puppy's three heads all looked at Zeus. Then they all looked at Hades. Then they all wagged their tails. Hades said: 'I see.' He made a note in his formal visiting scroll.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. COMEDY BEAT — The three-headed puppy sitting in front of Zeus and Hades. Each head is small, round, adorable — different expression on each (curious, sleepy, excited). Zeus crouching down with enormous delight, pointing at each head: 'Fluffy. Fluffy. Also Fluffy.' Hades behind him, holding an enormous formal scroll, making an amendment with a quill. All three puppy heads wag simultaneously. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Fog
scenes.narration: By mid-morning, the lower two rooms of Olympus were grey. Not dark — grey. A thin underworld fog that had followed Hades up from below, seeping through the lowest corridors. Everything it touched looked slightly dramatic. Athena's scrolls had a melancholy quality. The breakfast dishes were atmospheric. Baby Hercules walked into the fog room and came out looking like he was starring in a poem. He liked it. Hades had not noticed any of this.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. The lower corridor of Olympus filled with a thin grey atmospheric fog — not scary, just dramatic. Objects look slightly melancholy. Athena holding a scroll that now has a mournful quality despite containing a grocery list. Baby Hercules walks OUT of the fog room with a dramatically shadowed face looking like a tiny tragic hero, lion cape billowing slightly. He looks extremely pleased with his new aesthetic. Hades nearby, completely unaware, eating breakfast. 16:9.

scenes.title: Hades Discovers the Problem
scenes.narration: Athena showed Hades the fog. He looked at it. He was genuinely mortified. He had not known it followed him up. He said: 'I am so deeply sorry. This is inexcusable. I shall disperse it immediately.' He raised his hand. The fog pulled back slightly. He lowered his hand. It came back. He raised it again. The fog thinned. He blinked. He looked at his hand. He said: 'I may need a moment.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Hades standing at the corridor entrance with Athena, looking at the fog with genuine mortification. He raises his hand — fog thins. Lowers it — fog returns. Raises again — thins again. He stares at his own hand with dignified confusion. Athena watches this with patient analytical interest. The fog pulses gently with his hand movements. Three heads of Fluffy watch from below, fascinated. 16:9.

scenes.title: Sunlight and a Walk
scenes.narration: Athena's solution: Hades needed to walk in direct sunlight for ten minutes. The fog followed him — if he was above ground in the sun, it would dissipate. Hades looked at the sun. He put on two extra pairs of glasses. He said: 'Ten minutes.' He walked outside. He stood in the full Olympus sunlight with the expression of someone enduring a very bright but ultimately worthwhile experience. The fog slowly cleared. By minute seven, Hades said: 'It is actually quite warm.' By minute ten he said: 'Not entirely unpleasant.' This was, for Hades, extremely high praise.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. HADES standing in full Olympus sunlight, now wearing THREE pairs of round glasses stacked on his face, pale arms extended slightly, expression of dignified endurance. The thin fog visible behind him slowly clearing. The whole group standing nearby watching. At minute seven, a small smile begins. The Fluffy puppy is sitting on his foot, all three heads tilted up at the sun curiously. Golden outdoor scene. 16:9.

scenes.title: Hades Stays for Lunch
scenes.narration: Hades stayed for lunch. He described the sunlight as 'aggressively hospitable' and said Olympus had 'an excess of cheerfulness that was, on reflection, not entirely without merit.' Zeus said that was the nicest thing anyone had ever said about his home. Hades said he would return next year. He said Zeus could visit the underworld first if Zeus liked. Zeus said absolutely yes and immediately, and then looked at Athena, and Athena slowly shook her head. Zeus said perhaps next year.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Lunch scene — Hades at the table, three glasses removed now, slightly more relaxed, sitting with Zeus and the group. He's describing something formally while Zeus listens with complete warmth. Fluffy puppy under the table eating from three bowls simultaneously. Athena slowly shaking her head at Zeus off to the side. Achilles at the end of the table, eating, watching Hades with mild fascination. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: And that's the story of Uncle Hades's first visit to Olympus. He comes back every year now. He still wears three pairs of glasses for the first hour. He still describes things as 'not entirely unpleasant.' The fog doesn't follow him anymore — he figured out how to leave it at the door. Fluffy still has three names. See you next time on Little Olympus!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — Hades at the Olympus steps preparing to leave, three-headed puppy at his feet, Zeus waving warmly. Hades does something unprecedented: a small wave back. His expression is as close to cheerful as Hades gets, which looks like a very formal half-smile. The Olympus Sea visible in the background — sparkling, Poseidon's dolphins visible. Golden afternoon. 16:9.

lesson: People who seem serious or gloomy are often just in an unfamiliar place. Give them ten minutes in the sunlight.
hades_canon: Hades visits annually from EP009 forward. Always three pairs of glasses. Always calls things 'not entirely unpleasant.' Fluffy (all three heads) is now an Olympus regular.
youtube_title: Uncle HADES Visits Olympus (He Has THOUGHTS About Sunlight) ☀️ | Little Olympus Episode 9
next_episode_preview: Episode 10 — Something Rumbles Underground! And not in a good way. The ground is shaking, and it's not Hercules this time.
